commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - No narrow automation blocker remains; the next action is maintainer review of the new provider surface and availability risk.; Cleared: Cleared: the diff is security-sensitive because it provisions Windows guests, but I found no concrete credential leak or hidden supply-chain change beyond the documented guest bootstrap path. (sha=21c265dc6304a81aad33008ed88559ea70c7f495)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Hyper-V SSH-lease provider with config, CLI/env/YAML wiring, Windows VM provisioning and cleanup, registration, tests, and provider documentation.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a bug report. The relevant confidence signal is the PR body's live terminal smoke for the new provider, not a failing current-main reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - No narrow automation blocker remains; the next action is maintainer review of the new provider surface and availability risk.

target.title: feat: add Hyper-V provider for local Windows VMs
target.body:
## Summary

A `hyperv` provider for running Crabbox leases on local Windows VMs via Microsoft Hyper-V and PowerShell Direct — the Windows-native counterpart to `tart` (macOS), with no external CLI or cloud account. The design goal is "bring a plain Windows VHDX with a known administrator password, and it just works": the provider supplies everything else.

## How it works

`crabbox warmup --provider hyperv --hyperv-image <vhdx>`:

1. **Thin clone, not a copy.** Each lease is backed by a *differencing disk* over the template (`New-VHD -Differencing -ParentPath`) — near-instant and space-thin (a few MB up front), so the template stays read-only and shared instead of copying tens of GB per lease.
2. **Create + place off the system drive.** `New-VM -Generation 2 -Path <vmDir>` puts the VM config and the multi-GB `.VMRS` runtime files on the same (data) drive as the VHD rather than defaulting to `C:\ProgramData`, and automatic checkpoints are disabled (`Set-VM -AutomaticCheckpointsEnabled $false`) so a lease never spawns a `.avhdx` that would strand a disk on release.
3. **Make the guest reachable.** Over PowerShell Direct (authenticating with the guest admin password, passed via env — never argv) the provider installs the OpenSSH server if absent (`Add-WindowsCapability`, `Start-Service sshd`, firewall) and installs git (portable MinGit) if absent — mirroring how the Linux cloud-init path provisions a guest. Both are no-ops when already present, so a template that pre-bakes them just skips the download.
4. **Inject the lease key.** The SSH public key is written to the user's `authorized_keys` and, for admin accounts, `administrators_authorized_keys` with the SYSTEM+Administrators-only ACL that Windows OpenSSH requires (otherwise sshd silently ignores it).
5. **Wait for readiness**, then run/sync/release normally. On release the VM is removed and only the lease's own differencing disk + per-VM config dir are deleted; the template is untouched. Cleanup is scoped to the `crabbox-` name prefix.

Net template requirement: **a Generation-2 Windows VHDX whose administrator password is known to Crabbox** (`--hyperv-user` / `CRABBOX_HYPERV_GUEST_PASSWORD`). The provider does the rest. Non-Windows targets are rejected (from CLI, YAML, or env), and ISO images are rejected.

## Live proof — Windows 11 Pro, Hyper-V, Go 1.26.4

End-to-end on a **plain** MS dev VHDX (only an admin password set), C: deliberately full so storage is redirected to D: (which also exercises the off-system-drive placement):

```
$ crabbox doctor --provider hyperv
ok  provider  provider=hyperv hyperv=Enabled inventory=ready api=powershell leases=0 image=… ssh_probe=unchecked

$ crabbox warmup --provider hyperv --hyperv-image D:\…\win11.vhdx --hyperv-cpu 2 --hyperv-memory 4096
provisioned lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-hvfull-… state=ready
ready ssh=Administrator@172.25.78.240:22 network=public workroot=C:\crabbox
  (lease differencing disk created at ~4 MB — no multi-GB copy; VM config + .VMRS on D:)

$ crabbox run --provider hyperv --id cbx_… -- git --version
sync complete in 12.2s; command complete            # repo synced + command run over SSH

$ crabbox list --provider hyperv
crabbox-hvfull-… ready hyperv 172.25.78.240 lease=cbx_… slug=hvfull keep=true target=windows

$ crabbox stop --provider hyperv --id cbx_…
released lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-hvfull-…
# verify: crabbox- VMs remaining: 0   (VM, differencing disk, and config dir all removed)
```

Reaching `state=ready` exercises the full chain — OpenSSH install, git install, ACL-correct key injection, and the Windows readiness check (`git`/`tar`/work-root over SSH) — on a template that started with nothing but a password.

## Configuration

CLI: `--hyperv-image` (required), `--hyperv-cpu`, `--hyperv-memory`, `--hyperv-switch`, `--hyperv-user`, `--hyperv-work-root`. YAML `hyperv:` and `CRABBOX_HYPERV_*` env equivalents, incl. `CRABBOX_HYPERV_GUEST_PASSWORD`.

## Not included (follow-up)

- A configurable storage root (today storage follows `%USERPROFILE%`).
- ISO install / autounattend (templates are pre-installed VHDX).
- WSL2 mode (native Windows only).
target.author: anagnorisis2peripeteia

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

## How it works

`crabbox warmup --provider hyperv --hyperv-image <vhdx>`:

1. **Thin clone, not a copy.** Each lease is backed by a *differencing disk* over the template (`New-VHD -Differencing -ParentPath`) — near-instant and space-thin (a few MB up front), so the template stays read-only and shared instead of copying tens of GB per lease.
2. **Create + place off the system drive.** `New-VM -Generation 2 -Path <vmDir>` puts the VM config and the multi-GB `.VMRS` runtime files on the same (data) drive as the VHD rather than defaulting to `C:\ProgramData`, and automatic checkpoints are disabled (`Set-VM -AutomaticCheckpointsEnabled $false`) so a lease never spawns a `.avhdx` that would strand a disk on release.
3. **Make the guest reachable.** Over PowerShell Direct (authenticating with the guest admin password, passed via env — never argv) the provider installs the OpenSSH server if absent (`Add-WindowsCapability`, `Start-Service sshd`, firewall) and installs git (portable MinGit) if absent — mirroring how the Linux cloud-init path provisions a guest. Both are no-ops when already present, so a template that pre-bakes them just skips the download.
4. **Inject the lease key.** The SSH public key is written to the user's `authorized_keys` and, for admin accounts, `administrators_authorized_keys` with the SYSTEM+Administrators-only ACL that Windows OpenSSH requires (otherwise sshd silently ignores it).
5. **Wait for readiness**, then run/sync/release normally. On release the VM is removed and only the lease's own differencing disk + per-VM config dir are deleted; the template is untouched. Cleanup is scoped to the `crabbox-` name prefix.

Net template requirement: **a Generation-2 Windows VHDX whose administrator password is known to Crabbox** (`--hyperv-user` / `CRABBOX_HYPERV_GUEST_PASSWORD`). The provider does the rest. Non-Windows targets are rejected (from CLI, YAML, or env), and ISO images are rejected.

## Live proof — Windows 11 Pro, Hyper-V, Go 1.26.4

End-to-end on a **plain** MS dev VHDX (only an admin password set), C: deliberately full so storage is redirected to D: (which also exercises the off-system-drive placement):

```
$ crabbox doctor --provider hyperv
ok  provider  provider=hyperv hyperv=Enabled inventory=ready api=powershell leases=0 image=… ssh_probe=unchecked

$ crabbox warmup --provider hyperv --hyperv-image D:\…\win11.vhdx --hyperv-cpu 2 --hyperv-memory 4096
provisioned lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-hvfull-… state=ready
ready ssh=Administrator@172.25.78.240:22 network=public workroot=C:\crabbox
  (lease differencing disk created at ~4 MB — no multi-GB copy; VM config + .VMRS on D:)

$ crabbox run --provider hyperv --id cbx_… -- git --version
sync complete in 12.2s; command complete            # repo synced + command run over SSH

$ crabbox list --provider hyperv
crabbox-hvfull-… ready hyperv 172.25.78.240 lease=cbx_… slug=hvfull keep=true target=windows

$ crabbox stop --provider hyperv --id cbx_…
released lease=cbx_… instance=crabbox-hvfull-…
# verify: crabbox- VMs remaining: 0   (VM, differencing disk, and config dir all removed)
```

Reaching `state=ready` exercises the full chain — OpenSSH install, git install, ACL-correct key injection, and the Windows readiness check (`git`/`tar`/work-root over SSH) — on a template that started with nothing but a password.

## Configuration

CLI: `--hyperv-image` (required), `--hyperv-cpu`, `--hyperv-memory`, `--hyperv-switch`, `--hyperv-user`, `--hyperv-work-root`. YAML `hyperv:` and `CRABBOX_HYPERV_*` env equivalents, incl. `CRABBOX_HYPERV_GUEST_PASSWORD`.

## Not included (follow-up)

- A configurable storage root (today storage follows `%USERPROFILE%`).
- ISO install / autounattend (templates are pre-installed VHDX).
- WSL2 mode (native Windows only).
